specs.notes: NO/NO2/NOx via molybdenum converter. Standard workhorse ambient NOx monitor; molybdenum converter measures NOx (not true NO2).

Pulsed UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer; standard ambient configuration.

iQ-series pulsed UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer; successor to 43i. LOD value not independently verified.
Gas filter correlation IR CO analyzer; standard ambient configuration. Manufacturer LDL 0.04 ppm.

iQ-series gas filter correlation CO analyzer; successor to 48i. LOD value not independently verified.
Dual-cell UV absorption (254 nm) ozone analyzer; standard ambient configuration.

iQ-series dual-cell UV absorption ozone analyzer; successor to 49i. LOD value not independently verified.

Confirmed real. Direct methane and non-methane hydrocarbon (NMHC) analyzer using flame ionization detection with a back-flush GC column (eight-port rotary valve) to separate methane from NMHC; ~70 s measurement cycle. Draft's 'catalytic' separation description corrected to GC back-flush. Used in ambient and CEMS applications.

specs.notes: Confirmed real. NDIR CO2 analyzer using fixed optical bandpass interference filters plus quantum detection - NOT gas-filter-correlation (draft principle corrected). Measures CO2 only (not CH4) in ambient and source/stack emissions. iQ-series successor 410iQ also exists. Less common in ambient research than LI-COR/Picarro; more a source/stack CO2 monitor.

Predecessor to 43i/43i-TLE. specs.notes: Legacy C-series non-dispersive infrared gas-filter-correlation CO analyzer (4.6 um). -TL = trace-level variant (EPA index also lists an enhanced 48C-TLE). Predecessor to the 48i. specs.notes: Legacy C-series UV photometric (254 nm absorption) ozone analyzer. The 49C-PS is the ozone photometric primary/transfer-standard (calibrator) variant. Predecessor to the 49i. Used with a zero-air supply to produce calibration/audit gases for ambient analyzers. Not EPA method-designated (calibration equipment).

Commonly paired with the 146-series dynamic gas calibrator. Support equipment, not EPA method-designated.

Zero air supply providing clean dilution/reference air for calibration systems - VERIFIED as a genuine Thermo product (external-compressor unit; Purafil/potassium-permanganate + activated-charcoal scrubbers + ~350 C catalytic reactor to remove NO/NO2/O3/SO2/CO/HC). Flows up to ~20 L/min. Discontinued legacy model, succeeded by the 111iQ. Support equipment, not EPA method-designated. (Initial low-confidence doubt about existence was refuted by manufacturer documentation.)

specs.ranges: 0-50 ppb to 0-20 ppm full scale (dual/auto range, user selectable)

CO by non-dispersive infrared gas filter correlation. Current-generation successor to the Model 300E. US EPA RFM RFCA-1093-093 verified (EPA lists T300/T300U jointly).
Trace-level CO (GFC NDIR) with auto-reference feature, for NCore/low-background CO. EPA lists the T300/T300U jointly under Reference Method RFCA-1093-093, so the shared designation is legitimate.
O3 by UV absorption photometry at 254 nm. Current-generation successor to the Model 400E. US EPA EQM EQOA-0992-087 verified.

CO2 by non-dispersive infrared gas filter correlation (2.56 m folded-path sample cell), ambient to percent-level. Measures CO2 only (not CH4); mapped to 'CO2/CH4 analyzer' as the nearest enum. Confirmed real.
Higher-sensitivity variant of the T360 CO2 NDIR-GFC analyzer. Confirmed real (T360U addendum manual PN 07273A). Measures CO2 only; 'CO2/CH4 analyzer' is the nearest enum mapping.

specs.ranges: NOx 0-50 ppb to 0-20 ppm; O3 0-50 ppb to 0-1000 ppb (independent ranges)

principle: mass-flow dynamic dilution (with optional gas-phase titration)

Self-contained zero-air source with oil-free pump, drying/filtering and charcoal/purafil scrubbers that remove SO2, NO, NO2, O3, H2S and NH3; supplies dilution air for calibrators, permeation-oven purge air, and FID burner air. Older-generation UV-absorption (mercury-lamp) ozone monitor; predecessor of O342M; listed in EIONET measurement-equipment vocabulary. Model existence confirmed. Any US-EPA designation number not confirmed here, left null.

specs.ranges: selectable up to 0-10 ppm; EPA-designated range 0-500 ppb
specs.notes: UV-photometric (253.7 nm mercury lamp) ozone analyzer, 19-inch rack module. Widely used in 2006-2020 AQ networks. EPA equivalent-method designation confirmed.

Eco-designed e-series ozone analyzer using patented LED-based UV photometry at 253.7 nm (replaces the mercury lamp) - claim independently confirmed on ENVEA product materials; ultra-low power, 24 V option. QAL1 certified, US-EPA approved. Exact US-EPA number not confidently pinned, left null.
Older-generation infrared gas-filter-correlation CO monitor; predecessor of CO12M. EPA reference-method designation confirmed (1995).
Non-dispersive infrared gas-filter-correlation CO analyzer, 19-inch rack; optional CO2 channel. Common 2006-2020. EPA reference-method designation confirmed.

Eco-designed e-series NDIR CO analyzer, ISO 4224 compliant; optional CO2 measurement; QAL1 certified. Current generation. EPA reference-method designation RFCA-0915-228 confirmed (Federal Register 11/19/2015; stated verbatim on ENVEA/distributor datasheets).

Extractive multi-gas NDIR gas-filter-correlation CEMS measuring up to ~10 gases simultaneously (NO, NO2, SO2, CO, CO2, N2O, CH4, TOC, HCl, HF) plus O2. Variants: MIR 9000 CLD (adds chemiluminescence NOx), MIR 9000e, MIR 9000H, MIR 9000P. Over 4,000 installed. QAL1 certified; compliant with 40 CFR 60/75 and EU IED. Stack-emissions/CEMS instrument, not ambient - correctly not on EPA ambient NAAQS list.

Automated on-line gas chromatograph with photo-ionization detector for BTEX; measures benzene, toluene, ethylbenzene, m+p-xylene, o-xylene, 1,3-butadiene; ~15-min cycle. EN 14662-3 and EN 15267 (MCERTS/QAL1) compliant. Newer variant: VOC72e (confirmed to exist).

principle: dynamic dilution / gas-phase titration / UV ozone generation

specs.notes: Multi-gas dynamic-dilution calibrator with two thermal mass-flow controllers, internal UV ozone generator (NIST-traceable, photo-optical feedback for lamp aging) and a gas-phase titration (GPT) chamber. Performs zero/span/precision/multi-point calibration of NO/NO2/NOx, SO2, CO, O3 and hydrocarbon analyzers; meets US-EPA calibration requirements. Description confirmed against ENVEA materials.

specs.ranges: 0-250,000 ppb (0-250 ppm) selectable; typical use 0-250 ppb
specs.lod: Precision greater of 1.5 ppb or 2% of reading
specs.notes: Single-cell UV absorption at 254 nm. Lightweight (~2 kg), low power; widely used for portable and network ambient ozone monitoring. Averaging times selectable 10 s to hours. EPA FEM designation EQOA-0410-190 confirmed.

specs.ranges: 0-250,000 ppb (0-250 ppm) selectable; typical 0-250 ppb / 0-2000 ppb
specs.lod: 2.0 ppb; precision greater of 1.0 ppb or 2% of reading (10-s avg)
specs.notes: Dual-beam design measures reference and sample cells simultaneously for improved precision (~1 ppb) and faster response (~4 s, 2-s measurement frequency) than single-beam Model 202. 254 nm UV absorption. Shares EPA FEM designation EQOA-0410-190 with the Model 202 (approved as a modification of the 202). CORRECTED: draft's EQOA-0815-227 is actually the POM's designation, not the 205's.

principle: UV photometry with gas-phase (NO titration) scrubber

specs.notes: Scrubberless design: instead of a solid-phase (e.g. MnO2) scrubber, ~5 ppm NO is pulsed into the sample every ~2 s to titrate ozone (NO + O3 -> NO2 + O2) as the reference, removing interference from UV-absorbing species. ~30 cm path. Best suited to heavily polluted air where VOC/mercury/particulate interferences are likely. Principle confirmed on manufacturer datasheet.

specs.ranges: 0-100 ppm (Low range of the 106 industrial/ambient series)
specs.notes: 106 series 'ozone monitor on a board' for elevated/process ozone. -L = Low range (0-100 ppm), the lowest of the L/M/MH/H family. Range confirmed on manufacturer/distributor listings. Draft's 1.5 ppb LOD and 2 s response nulled as implausible/unverified for a 0-100 ppm instrument.

-M = Medium range (0-1,000 ppm) variant of the 106 ozone monitor series, for higher-concentration ozone (ozone generation/disinfection). Range confirmed on manufacturer/distributor listings; draft's 2 s response nulled as unverified.

-MH = Medium-High range (0-10,000 ppm) variant of the 106 series for high-concentration ozone (ozone generators, industrial). Distinct from the 106-H (High, 0-20 wt%). Not used for ambient air. Range confirmed on manufacturer/distributor listings; draft's 2 s response nulled as unverified.

specs.ranges: LOD ~4 ppb up to 0-10 ppm; typical 0-1000 ppb
specs.lod: 4 ppb (precision greater of ~1.5 ppb or 2% of reading)
specs.notes: Miniature (~340 g / 0.75 lb) wearable ozone monitor with built-in GPS; based on the dual-beam UV technology of the Model 205. Used for personal exposure and mobile/spatial ozone mapping. EPA FEM designation EQOA-0815-227 confirmed. Draft's LOD of 1.5 ppb is actually the precision; manufacturer LOD is ~4 ppb.

principle: Direct optical absorption at 405 nm for NO2; NO by O3 conversion of NO to NO2 (by difference); NOx = NO + NO2

specs.notes: NO2 measured directly by absorbance at 405 nm (~2 m folded optical path); NO measured by adding ozone to convert NO to NO2 and taking the difference; NOx = NO + NO2. Avoids molybdenum/photolytic-converter efficiency issues of chemiluminescence NOx monitors. Principle confirmed on manufacturer datasheet.

principle: Ozone-based measurement (NO + O3 -> NO2 + O2; resulting ozone depletion measured by UV absorption at 254 nm)

specs.notes: CORRECTED PRINCIPLE: Model 410 NO monitor measures NO by reacting it with added ozone and detecting the resulting ozone depletion by UV absorbance at 254 nm (same UV/ozone-monitor platform, an absolute method requiring infrequent calibration) - it is NOT the 405 nm NO2-detection platform as the draft stated. Replaces the Model 400 (~2x better precision). With the Model 401 NO2 Converter, measures NOx and NO2 (by difference). Model confirmed to exist via manufacturer brochure/manual; draft under-confidence corrected upward.

specs.lod: ~0.05 ppb (50 ppt) at 3 s; down to ~5 ppt at 60 s in high-sensitivity config
specs.notes: Scientific-line ('Tropospheric Research') fast-response NO chemiluminescence analyzer for free-troposphere / aircraft / vertical-flux work. Manufacturer-stated sensitivity 50 ppt in 3 s and 10 ppt in 60 s (25 ppt/3 s or 5 ppt/60 s enhanced). Used with molybdenum or PLC photolytic converters for NO2. Response time not published as a discrete time-constant, so left null.

specs.lod: low-ppt (order of a few ppt) in ppt configuration; exact value config-dependent
specs.notes: High-sensitivity ppt-level NO chemiluminescence analyzer widely cited in pre-2010 atmospheric field/aircraft campaigns; frequently paired with the Eco Physics PLC 760 photolytic converter (and heated-molybdenum converters) for NO2. LOD softened to an order-of-magnitude claim rather than a single fixed figure since it depends on integration time and configuration.

Classic Eco Physics NO chemiluminescence detector (700-series; documented via 'Brochure CLD 700 AL' and secondary-market listings). Used with molybdenum/photolytic NO2 converters. Note: a distinct 'CLD 700 AL ppt' designation could not be confirmed and was removed from aliases (the ppt-grade model in the literature is the CLD 770 AL ppt); do not conflate with the source-emissions CLD 700 EL.

Compact chemiluminescence NO/NOx analyzer; CLD 88 p (paired with photolytic converter PLC 860) and CLD 88 Y variants documented (e.g. 'CLD 88 Y p' brochure; CLD 88Y in institutional equipment lists). Now largely superseded by the modular nCLD 88 p. Range/LOD withheld pending confident datasheet values.

Chemiluminescence NO/NO2/NOx analyzer in the 800 Series; documented variants include CLD 844 CM hr (adds NH3/amines via dual converters) and the modular nCLD 844 Mh. Used in ambient/combustion and ammonia-slip monitoring. Removed unverified alias 'CLD 844 S'. Specs vary by configuration and are withheld.

specs.lod: ~0.4 ppb (manufacturer min. detectable concentration, ambient config)
specs.notes: Two-channel modular ('n'-series) chemiluminescence NO/NO2/NOx analyzer conforming to EN 14211 for ambient NOx; manufacturer states min. detectable ~0.4 ppb (ambient-level, not a ppt flux instrument). Targets ambient/indoor/clean-room/atmospheric-research use; can be combined with a PLC 860 photolytic converter for interference-free NO2 in clean air. Removed unverified aliases 'nCLD 855 AL' and 'nCLD 855 Y hr'.

Supreme-line modular chemiluminescence NO/NO2/NOx analyzer for ppt-level research; documented variants nCLD 899 Y and nCLD 899 CY (CY additionally handles NOx-amines/NH3). 'nCLD 899' used here as the series label. Datasheet numeric specs withheld pending confident values.

CAUTION: this is a photolytic NO2-to-NO converter ACCESSORY, not a standalone analyzer; categorized as 'NO2 analyzer' only because that is the closest enum option. Confirmed current auxiliary product (PLC 860); can convert NO2 down to a few ppt and avoids the HONO/NOy interference of molybdenum converters. Placed ahead of Eco Physics CLD/nCLD detectors to give interference-free true NO2. Older PLC 760 form appears in atmospheric literature (e.g. paired with CLD 770 AL ppt).

specs.ranges: CO2 0-1000 ppm; CH4 0-20 ppm; CO 0-5000 ppb (0-5 ppm); H2O 0-7% (standard datasheet ranges)
specs.notes: Simultaneous CO, CO2, CH4, H2O. Guaranteed 1-sigma precision (5 s / 5 min) per datasheet: CO <15 / <1.5 ppb; CO2 <50 / <20 ppb; CH4 <1 / <0.5 ppb; H2O <30 / <5 ppm. Automatic H2O correction reports dry-gas mole fractions. Meets WMO/ICOS ambient GHG requirements. Airborne variant sold as G2401-m. Confirmed against manufacturer datasheet.

specs.ranges: CO2 0-1000 ppm; CH4 0-20 ppm; H2O 0-7% (standard datasheet ranges)
specs.notes: Simultaneous CO2, CH4, H2O (no CO). WMO/ICOS-grade; automatic H2O correction reports dry mole fractions. Flight/mobile variant G2301-m. NOTE: the fast eddy-covariance flux analyzer is the separate G2311-f, not a 'G2301-f' (drafted 'G2301-f fast/flux variant' removed as unverified). Precision figures vary by datasheet revision; not treated as guaranteed here.

Confirmed real Picarro model (CO2/CO/H2O analyzer; user guide and datasheet exist). Simultaneous CO2, CO, H2O (adds CO and drops CH4 relative to the G2301). H2O correction reports dry-gas mole fractions. Marketed as meeting WMO CO2/CO precision objectives. Now a legacy line, largely superseded by the G2401 (which adds CH4); commercial status uncertain. Numeric datasheet ranges/precision not confidently recalled, left null.

Simultaneous N2O, CH4, CO2, NH3, H2O. Precision (1 min / 5 min) per datasheet: N2O <10 / <5 ppb; CH4 <7 / <5 ppb; CO2 <300 / <200 ppb; NH3 <3 / <1 ppb (confirmed against manufacturer datasheet). Designed for soil/agriculture flux and chamber (open or closed) systems; automatic water correction reports dry mole fractions. Numeric measurement ranges not confidently recalled, left null.

specs.lod: Lower detectable limit <0.07 ppb (0.05 ug/m3) best case per datasheet; 5-min precision ~30 ppt (the ~30/~170 ppt figures are precision, not detection limits)
specs.notes: Ambient ammonia (NH3) analyzer. Precision 1 s / 10 s / 5 min = 500 / 170 / 30 ppt (confirmed against datasheet). Zero drift +/-0.15 ppb over 72 h at STP; rise/fall time <2 min for a 20 ppb NH3 challenge. Measures/corrects H2O to report dry-basis NH3; no drying required. Earlier draft framed precision values as the LOD; corrected to distinguish precision from the datasheet's stated lower detectable limit.

specs.lod: sub-ppb (5 min precision <40 ppt for N2O and CO)
specs.notes: Simultaneous N2O, CO, H2O. Precision (5 s / 5 min) per datasheet: N2O <200 / <40 ppt; CO <200 / <40 ppt; drift <100 ppt over 24 h. Meets WMO/ICOS requirements for N2O and CO; static installations. Succeeded by the upgraded PI5310 (built on the G5310 with newer mid-IR CRDS; 1-1500 ppb range, <5 s), which is the current catalog model rather than a pure rename.

specs.ranges: CO2 0-1000 ppmv; CH4 0-10 ppmv; H2O reported (per G1301 user manual)
specs.notes: First-generation field GHG analyzer (~2008-2011), simultaneous CO2, CH4, H2O; formerly branded EnviroSense 3000i. WS-CRDS at ~5 Hz; reports dry mole fractions without drying. Airborne variant G1301-c flown on NASA aircraft; mobile/flux variants also produced. CORRECTION: G1301 manual specifies CH4 0-10 ppmv (draft's 0-20 ppm was too high). Discontinued (superseded by G2301) but dominant in pre-2012 GHG and eddy-covariance literature.

model_aliases: LGR Greenhouse Gas Analyzer (CH4, CO2, H2O)

specs.ranges: CH4 0.01-100 ppm (Extended Range to ~10%); CO2 200-20000 ppm; H2O to ~7% (upper H2O range varies by source, some cite ~30000 ppm)
specs.lod: 1-sigma precision (5 s): CH4 ~1 ppb, CO2 ~150 ppb, H2O ~100 ppm
specs.notes: Rackmount/benchtop CH4/CO2/H2O analyzer, measurement rates up to 10 Hz; heavily used for eddy-covariance and airborne flux. Reports dry mole fractions via measured H2O. Ordering numbers (fast flow 907-0010 / benchtop 908-0010, EP 911-0010/911-0011) are commonly cited but not individually verified here. Removed a datasheet URL on a Russian reseller mirror (lgrinc.ru) that could not be confirmed.

specs.ranges: CH4 0.01-100 ppm; CO2 200-20000 ppm; H2O up to ~70000 ppm
specs.lod: 1-sigma precision (1 s) ~ CH4 2 ppb, CO2 0.3 ppm (approx; datasheet-dependent)
specs.notes: Compact, crushproof field-portable version of the GGA (CH4/CO2/H2O), ~70 W, TSA-carry-on size; measurement rate up to 1 Hz. Model 915-0011 (ABB LGR user manual confirmed); rebranded by ABB as GLA132-GGA (verified). Removed dubious alias 'UPGA 915-0011' (non-standard abbreviation, not a verified literature form). Precision figures approximate.

specs.ranges: N2O and CO each linear/tested to ~2 ppm (per ABB GLA151-N2OCM datasheet); typical ambient N2O ~330 ppb, ambient CO tens-hundreds of ppb; H2O to saturation
specs.lod: 1-sigma precision (ABB GLA151-N2OCM): N2O 0.3 ppb / CO 0.3 ppb at 1 s; N2O 0.04 ppb / CO 0.05 ppb at 100 s
specs.notes: Simultaneous N2O, CO (and H2O); reports dry mole fractions. Current ABB rebrand GLA151-N2OCM is a QC-Portable OA-ICOS analyzer (verified). CORRECTED: draft's 1 s precision (CO ~0.05 ppb, N2O ~0.08 ppb) were actually the ~100 s figures mislabeled as 1 s; true 1 s precision is ~0.3 ppb each per datasheet. Removed fabricated accuracy spec (+/-2 ppb or +/-2%), the >5 ppm CO range (datasheet linear tested to ~2 ppm), unverified legacy order numbers 907-0015/908-0014, and an unverifiable ARM report number. Response time set to datasheet gas-flow 1/e (8 s, standard pump). Categorized multi-gas because N2O has no dedicated category.

specs.lod: ~0.2 ppb (1 s) for the trace/standard configuration; economical variant is less sensitive
specs.notes: Off-axis ICOS ammonia (and H2O) analyzer for atmospheric NH3 monitoring; up to ~10 Hz nominal (inlet adsorption lengthens effective NH3 response, so response_time left null). Model EAA-22 (Economical Ammonia Analyser) VERIFIED via CEDA/MAQS instrument record; ABB rebrand GLA331-EAA (Enhanced-Performance rackmount) VERIFIED. Range/precision figures apply to the general LGR NH3 analyzer and may differ between economical and EP units.

model_aliases: Los Gatos Research methane analyzer (CH4, H2O)

specs.ranges: CH4 ~0.01-100 ppm (higher in landfill/flux configs, to ~1000 ppm); H2O optional
specs.lod: CH4 ~1-2 ppb (1-sigma, ~1 s); FMA fast variant cited at ~0.1 ppb-class precision at up to 10-20 Hz; exact figure configuration-dependent
specs.notes: Early LGR off-axis ICOS platform measuring CH4 (H2O optional); the fast-flow variant (Fast Methane Analyzer, FMA) with scroll pump reaches ~0.10 s cell response and 10 Hz, widely used for eddy-covariance CH4 flux pre-2012. DLT-100 = FMA VERIFIED via CEDA record and multiple EC papers. Standard unit measures CH4 only (categorized CO2/CH4 as nearest fit). Removed unverified order number 908-0001. Historically important; largely superseded by FGGA/UGGA.

specs.ranges: CH4 0.01-100 ppm (Extended Range option to % level); CO2 200-20000 ppm; H2O
specs.notes: Smallest/lightest of the GGA family (~5.4 kg, <35 W), measures CH4, CO2 (and H2O on newer units); battery-portable for mobile/survey and leak detection. Model M-GGA-918 VERIFIED (LGR datasheet) and ABB rebrand GLA131-GGA VERIFIED (Microportable). Precision figures not confidently known (LOD left null).

model_aliases: Cavity Attenuated Phase Shift NO2 monitor

specs.ranges: 0-3000 ppbv (0-3 ppm), configurable; fast-response version available
specs.lod: <0.1 ppb (<100 ppt at 10 s; ~1 ppb on 1 s fast version)
specs.notes: Cavity Attenuated Phase Shift spectroscopy, blue LED ~450 nm (20 nm bandpass ~440 nm); direct NO2 absorption, no converter needed. Widely used for direct NO2 in field campaigns. Precision (2-sigma, 1 s) <0.5 ppb; LOD/response depend on averaging. Confirmed dedicated Aerodyne product with published datasheet.

specs.lod: sub-ppb (NO2 channel ~0.1 ppb / <100 ppt at 10 s)
specs.notes: CAPS-based NOx variant: measures NO2 directly by CAPS; total NOx via ozone conversion of NO to NO2 then CAPS detection, toggling conversion on/off to separate NO and NO2. Confirmed by Aerodyne 'CAPS NOx-NO2 Monitor' datasheet. Less common in literature than the pure NO2 CAPS.

model_aliases: Tunable Infrared Laser Direct Absorption Spectrometer
principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL/ICL)

specs.notes: Platform family (Mini single-laser, Dual-laser, Compact) using astigmatic Herriott/AMAC multipass cell with QC or IC lasers. Aerodyne lists configurable species: NO, N2O, NO2, NH3, CO, CO2, CH4, C2H6, COS, HCHO, O3, EtO and others. LOD/range species-dependent; ~1-10 Hz, eddy-covariance capable. Generic platform row; see species-specific rows.

principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL)

specs.lod: sub-ppb (~0.1 ppb; ~60-100 ppt at 1 s reported)
specs.notes: QCL-based direct-absorption NH3 monitor, noted for fast response and low inlet adsorption; used for ammonia flux (eddy covariance) and ambient monitoring. NH3 is a confirmed TILDAS species. 'TILDAS NH3' is a functional config label rather than a catalog SKU (sold as TILDAS Mini/Compact configured for NH3). LOD depends on configuration/averaging.

principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL)

specs.notes: QCL direct-absorption N2O analyzer, often combined with CO or CO2; widely used for soil/agricultural N2O flux by eddy covariance. N2O is a confirmed TILDAS species. Some configurations also measure N2O isotopologues (site preference). Species-tagged config label, not an official SKU. (No dedicated N2O category in schema; classed as multi-gas.)

principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL/ICL)

specs.notes: Direct-absorption CH4 analyzer; dual-laser versions co-measure ethane (C2H6) for source attribution (natural gas leaks) - Aerodyne markets a TILDAS Compact single-laser ethane analyzer and CH4/C2H6 dual. Used for methane flux and mobile surveys. Config label, not an official SKU.

principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL)

specs.notes: QCL direct-absorption formaldehyde monitor; used in aircraft and ground campaigns as a fast HCHO reference. HCHO is a confirmed TILDAS species. LOD typically sub-ppb depending on averaging. Config label, not an official SKU. (No dedicated formaldehyde-analyzer category in schema; classed as multi-gas.)

principle: tunable IR laser direct absorption spectroscopy (QCL)

specs.notes: Direct-absorption CO analyzer, frequently in dual configurations with N2O or CO2; used as combustion tracer and for flux measurements. CO is a confirmed TILDAS species. Config label, not an official SKU.

Pulsed-UV fluorescence with PMT. Confirmed FPI product (directindustry / en.fpi-inc.com). Principle is the US-EPA-recommended reference method for trace SO2; note the instrument itself carries Chinese certification, not a US EPA designation. Widely deployed in China's national ambient network.

NO/NO2/NOx by chemiluminescence with catalytic/molybdenum NO2-to-NO converter. Confirmed FPI product (directindustry). Chemiluminescence is the US EPA reference method for NO2/NOx (describes the principle, not a US EPA designation for this model). Range/LOD identical to AQMS-500 in the draft — plausible trace-ambient values but not independently cross-checked against the datasheet.

UV photometric absorption at 254 nm with reference measurement via ozone scrubber; ppb-level O3. Confirmed FPI product (聚光科技 AQMS-300臭氧分析仪). Draft datasheet URL removed — it 302-redirects to a 404 page and does not resolve to a live product page.

Non-dispersive infrared with gas filter correlation (GFC wheel), long-path cell (~14 m). Confirmed FPI product (environmental-expert / directindustry). LOD is an approximate datasheet-class value typical of GFC CO analyzers.

Direct NO2 measurement by cavity attenuated phase shift (CAPS), avoiding the converter artefact of chemiluminescence NOx analyzers. Confirmed as a real FPI product. Range values are approximate/unverified against the datasheet.

Dynamic dilution multi-point calibrator confirmed on en.fpi-inc.com: two mass flow controllers (dilution 0–10 SLPM, standard 0–100 sccm), up to 4 calibration gas inputs, optional internal ozone generator (0.1–6 ppm output) and UV photometer for GPT/O3 calibration. QA/QC device, not a gas analyzer.
Ambient NH3 analyzer confirmed as an FPI product; NH3 oxidatively converted to NO via an external converter and detected by chemiluminescence, designed to pair with the AQMS-600 NOx analyzer.

Confirmed FPI product, but it is a CO2-ONLY analyzer (non-dispersive infrared, ppm level) — it does NOT measure CH4. Category label 'CO2/CH4 analyzer' is retained only as the closest available bucket; do not read it as methane capability.
Ambient H2S analyzer confirmed as an FPI product (datasheet available). H2S catalytically converted to SO2 (internal converter ~315 °C) after an SO2 scrubber, then detected by pulsed UV fluorescence.

Online VOC/hydrocarbon analyzer confirmed as an FPI product (environmental-expert / directindustry). Three-valve four-column GC-FID measuring methane, non-methane hydrocarbons (NMHC) and BTEX simultaneously; applied in both industrial-emission and ambient/urban VOC networks.

Portable FID methane / non-methane total hydrocarbon (THC/CH4/NMHC) analyzer confirmed as an FPI product; heated sampling line (up to ~180 °C) for stationary-source exhaust gas and fugitive/unorganized emissions. Draft claims of BTEX capability and specific 'HJ 1012-2018' compliance are unverified and removed; the invented 'EXPEC 3200-120' alias was dropped.

specs.lod: ~1 ppm (critical species, stack level; per datasheet)
specs.notes: Stack/flue-gas continuous emission monitoring system confirmed on en.fpi-inc.com (SO2, NOx, NO2, CO, CO2, O2; expandable to HCl, HF, H2S, dust). Hot-wet extractive sampling. Regulatory EMISSION monitoring, not ambient — included for completeness; not typically cited in ambient-air Methods sections. LOD ~1 ppm is stated on the datasheet for critical species.

Micro/mini air-quality station confirmed as an FPI product (environmental-expert 'AQMS-3000 Micro Air Quality Monitoring System') measuring SO2, NO, NOx, CO, O3, PM2.5, PM10 in a compact grid-monitoring enclosure. This is a gridded-network micro-station, NOT a single reference analyzer; sensor principles vary by channel and 'multi-gas analyzer' is only the closest available category.

model_aliases: XHAQMS3000型小型化空气质量连续监测系统
principle: Compact continuous ambient station housing UV-fluorescence SO2, chemiluminescence NO/NO2/NOx, UV-photometric O3 and GFC-NDIR CO analyzer modules (miniaturized standard-method configuration)

specs.ranges: Multi-gas system; module-dependent (SO2/NO2/O3 sub-ppb to ~ppm, CO ~ppm)
specs.notes: VERIFIED model: appears in Hebei Province首台(套) major-equipment catalog as 'XHAQMS3000型小型化空气质量连续监测系统' (compact/miniaturized continuous air-quality monitoring system). This is a station/system designation, not a single analyzer — Chinese station Methods sometimes cite the whole station as 'Sailhero/先河 XHAQMS'. Module principles listed are typical for a compact standard-method station but are inferred, not individually verified. XHAQMS-4000 is referenced as a mobile monitoring/calibration vehicle; 1000/2000 variants unverified. CORRECTED: the specific CCEP designation number in the draft could not be verified and was nulled — CCEP certification is plausible but the '质(认)字 No. 2018-233' string is unconfirmed.

model_aliases: 先河环保 网格化微型站
principle: Sensor-based TVOC micro-station using photoionization detector (PID) plus metal-oxide semiconductor sensor

specs.notes: VERIFIED model but CORRECTED scope: vendor product page describes XHAQSN-801 specifically as the 'TVOC微型站' measuring TVOC (plus temp/humidity) via PID + metal-oxide, NOT a multi-gas SO2/NO2/O3/CO station. The draft's electrochemical SO2/NO2/O3/CO description conflated it with sibling models — the six-parameter unit is XHAQSN-808 and XHAQSN-804 is the particulate variant. Low-cost gridded ('网格化') micro station, NOT a reference-grade analyzer; appears in Chinese gridded/low-cost-sensor studies. Category changed from 'multi-gas analyzer' to THC/VOC analyzer accordingly.

specs.notes: Ecotech EC9800-series NOx analyzer; EC9841B marketed on Hebei Sailhero product pages (Sailhero–Ecotech 2008 partnership). manufacturer: Hebei Sailhero (先河环保); OEM Ecotech (Australia)

principle: computerized mass-flow-controlled gas dilution with ozone generation and gas-phase titration

First-generation commercial UV photometric (254 nm absorption) ozone monitor; dominant in 1980s-1990s ambient O3 literature. EPA equivalent method EQOA-0577-019 covers variants 1003-AH/-PC/-RS (verified against EPA designated-methods list).

EPA equivalent method EQOA-0383-056 covers variants 1008-AH/-PC/-RS; successor/replacement units sold by Tanabyte Engineering. Draft alias '1008-HC' was unconfirmed and removed (EPA lists only -AH/-PC/-RS).

Gas-phase chemiluminescence oxides-of-nitrogen analyzer (NO/NO2/NOx via converter). EPA automated reference method RFNA-1192-089 (Federal Register 57 FR 55530, 25 Nov 1992), verified against EPA/CARB NOx analyzer lists.

Gas filter correlation nondispersive infrared (~4.6 um) CO analyzer. EPA automated reference method RFCA-0488-067 (verified against EPA designated-methods list). Draft range/LOD (0.1 ppm), response time (120 s) and linearity/drift figures were unverified for this exact model and nulled rather than guessed.

UV fluorescence SO2 analyzer with Teflon-coated particulate filter and continuous hydrocarbon-removal system. EPA-designated equivalent method EQSA-1086-061 (Oct 1986); confirmed real Dasibi SO2 model (range and features verified against EPA list; a used unit is also listed on the resale market).

Chemiluminescence oxides-of-nitrogen analyzer; among the earliest EPA-designated NOx reference methods. RFNA-0977-025 (Federal Register 42 FR 46574, 16 Sep 1977); designation also covers the related CSI Model 5600. CSI = Columbia Scientific Industries, Austin TX.

Current versions add a UV LED path for low-level SO2/NO2. Widely used in CEMS/stack monitoring and combustion research; auto-calibration with ambient air. Ranges are order-selectable so a single fixed spec cannot be given.

specs.ranges: Single/dual IR component (e.g. CO, CO2, NO, SO2, CH4); smallest spans down to a few ppm depending on component and cell length
specs.notes: High-sensitivity NDIR bench in rack (6E, up to 4 IR components) and field (6F, up to 2 IR components) housings. Common in emissions/process measurement; often paired with Oxymat 6 for O2. Ranges depend on configured component and optical path so no universal fixed value.

specs.notes: Heated total hydrocarbon (THC) FID analyzer for hot/wet sample gas. Part of Siemens Series 6. Used in emission monitoring of organic carbon (VOC/TOC) from stacks and process streams; also trace HC in high-purity gases.

specs.ranges: NDIR for CO, CO2, NO, SO2, CH4, N2O etc.; up to 4 components, smallest ranges e.g. CO 0-10 ppm, NO 0-30 ppm (configuration-dependent)
specs.notes: IR photometer module of the ABB Advance Optima AO2000 platform. Widely used in CEMS and combustion/atmospheric-chamber studies. Multiple components configurable so ranges vary by build.

principle: UV resonance absorption spectroscopy (UV-RAS) / differential UV photometry

specs.ranges: UV photometer for NO, NO2, SO2; low ranges e.g. NO 0-15 ppm, NO2 0-10 ppm, SO2 0-15 ppm (configuration-dependent)
specs.notes: UV analyzer module of the AO2000 platform, used for NO/NO2/SO2 in emission monitoring. Successor generations include Limas21/Limas23; the exact designation used in Methods varies by era.

specs.ranges: NDIR for CO, CO2, SO2, CH4, NO (measured directly); multi-component up to 5 gases plus O2 (fuel cell/zirconia); low ranges e.g. CO 0-50/200 ppm, SO2 0-200 ppm
specs.notes: Fuji Electric single-beam infrared gas analyzer Type ZRE, often combined with fuel-cell/zirconia O2. Common in Asian CEMS/stack and combustion studies (incinerators, boilers, furnaces). Exact ranges are order-configured. Note: ZRJ is a separate Fuji model, not an alias of ZRE.

specs.ranges: 0-10 ppm up to high percent-level (selectable, as CH4/propane)

Automated GC-FID with cryogen-free preconcentration for light ambient hydrocarbons (ethane through hexane), used for ozone-precursor monitoring in European air-quality networks; pairs with airmoVOC C6-C12. Confirmed on Chromatotec pages.

Portable 10.6 eV photoionization detector total-VOC monitor (ppm range, 0.1 ppm resolution), isobutylene-referenced; widely used for industrial and ambient VOC screening and leak surveys. Later marketed under Honeywell RAE Systems.
